title: dactylorhiza urvilleana subsp. ilgazica
intro: Dactylorhiza urvilleana subsp. ilgazica is a subspecies of orchid, commonly known as a type of orchid found in the Ilgaz Mountains of Turkey. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for various infections and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to stimulate blood flow, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Dactylorhiza urvilleana subsp. ilgazica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
